question_text: A boy eats 1200 kJ at breakfast and 1800 kJ at lunch. If his daily energy need is 9000 kJ, what fraction of his daily energy requirement has he used?
correct_answer: 1/3 (or 0.33 or 33%) - he has consumed 3000 kJ out of 9000 kJ
explanation: First add the breakfast and lunch energy: 1200 + 1800 = 3000 kJ. Then find what fraction this is of the daily total: 3000 ÷ 9000 = 1/3. This means he needs to consume 2/3 of his daily energy in dinner and snacks.
